comment_text.sn1.31:1.3: These deities appear only in this chapter. | “Felicity” renders ullāpa, which judging from the use in this chapter means “uplifting saying, felicity” rather than “praise”.

root_text.sn1.31:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn1.31:1.3: Atha kho sambahulā satullapakāyikā devatāyo abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.sn1.31:1.4: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho ekā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.31:3.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.31:5.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.31:7.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.31:9.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.31:11.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.31:13.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.31:14.1: “Sabbāsaṁ vo subhāsitaṁ pariyāyena, api ca mamapi suṇātha—
root_text.sn1.31:16.2: Attamanā tā devatāyo bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyiṁsūti.

translation_text.sn1.31:0.2: 4. The Host of a Hundred Felicities
translation_text.sn1.31:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.
translation_text.sn1.31:1.3: Then, late at night, several glorious deities of the host of a hundred felicities, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side.
translation_text.sn1.31:1.4: Standing to one side, one deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.31:2.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn1.31:2.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn1.31:3.1: Then another deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.31:4.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn1.31:4.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn1.31:4.4: wisdom is gained—but not from anyone else.”
translation_text.sn1.31:5.1: Then another deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.31:6.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn1.31:6.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn1.31:6.4: you grieve not among the grieving.”
translation_text.sn1.31:7.1: Then another deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.31:8.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn1.31:8.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn1.31:9.1: Then another deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.31:10.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn1.31:10.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn1.31:10.4: sentient beings go to a good place.”
translation_text.sn1.31:11.1: Then another deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.31:12.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn1.31:12.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn1.31:13.1: Then another deity said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn1.31:14.1: “You’ve all spoken well in your own way. However, listen to me also:
translation_text.sn1.31:15.2: Try to get close to the virtuous!
translation_text.sn1.31:15.3: Understanding the true teaching of the good,
translation_text.sn1.31:16.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn1.31:16.2: Then those deities, knowing that the teacher approved, bowed and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on their right, before vanishing right there.

reference_text.sn1.31:1.1: dr15.22, ms12S1_127, msdiv31, pts-vp-pli1ed1.17, sya15.24, vri23.20
